subject: docs(claude): icons/haptics/ModalBackdrop + pattern-audit + z-index rules
body:
Brings CLAUDE.md in sync with the Part A–D code changes of the
APP_ICONS + ModalBackdrop + haptics branch, and clears the three
findings I pushed back on during the earlier audit — those were
valid fleet-hygiene items, not scope creep, once the user confirmed
they wanted everything addressed.

- **§Icon Assets**: two-source table replacing the single-source
  table. Now lists `assets/icon-source-maskable.svg`,
  `public/icon-1024-maskable.png`, and `public/favicon.ico`
  alongside the existing any-purpose outputs. §Regenerating Icons
  updated to mention keeping the two SVGs in lockstep and
  references the `png-to-ico` favicon packer.

- **§Haptic Feedback** (new subsection under §Core Concepts next to
  Emoji Reactions): documents the `utils/haptics.ts` helper API,
  the HIG-aligned Light/Medium/Warning impact matrix for synctone's
  surfaces, the auto-haptic on `ModalBackdrop`, the error-swallow
  + logger.warn semantics, and the physical-device testing caveat.

- **§Pattern Adoption Status** (new top-level section right before
  §Not Applicable Patterns): classifies every glow-props pattern
  (all 10) as adopted / not-applicable / applicable-but-unimplemented,
  with the specific synctone surface or rationale for each. Closes
  the "6 unclassified patterns" gap honestly — PWA_ICON_CACHE_BUST
  and HTTPS_PROXY marked as unimplemented with revisit conditions;
  APP_ICONS, DEBUG_SYSTEM, PWA_SYSTEM, THEME_DARK_MODE, Z_INDEX_SCALE
  marked as adopted with file-path evidence.

- **§Z-Index Scale**: tier-usage table updated from "reserved" to
  actual consumers now that ModalBackdrop is real — `Z.backdrop`
  used by every modal's dismiss layer, `Z.modal` used by every
  modal's content wrapper, `Z.menu` used by ChatHeaderMenu
  dropdown. New rule #5 states the Android elevation-vs-zIndex
  footgun (native Android treats elevation and zIndex as separate
  stacking systems) with the current elevation inventory. New
  rule #6 covers the transform/filter/opacity stacking-context
  trap relevant to `MessageBubble`'s swipe-to-reply animation.

- **§Project Structure**: adds `components/common/ModalBackdrop.tsx`
  under the components/ tree and `utils/haptics.ts` under utils/.

Tags: documentation, pwa, haptics, fleet-alignment, patterns
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: fleet-alignment
Semver: patch
